title: Web Calling: User Guide
summary: Complete guide to using net2phone Web Calling. Covers setup, making and receiving calls from your browser, microphone permissions, and troubleshooting.
keywords: web calling, browser calling, softphone, make calls, receive calls

title: Web Calling: Video Tutorial
summary: Video walkthrough of net2phone Web Calling features. Step-by-step visual guide for getting started with browser-based calling.
keywords: web calling, video, tutorial, how to

title: Data vs. Minute Calling
summary: Explains the difference between data-based calling (VoIP over internet) and minute-based calling plans. Helps you choose the right plan for your usage.
keywords: data calling, minute calling, plans, VoIP

title: Enabling and Disabling Web Calling: Getting Started
summary: How to enable or disable Web Calling for your account or individual users. Admin settings and user preferences.
keywords: enable, disable, web calling, settings, admin

title: How To Setup Your Call Forwarding
summary: Step-by-step instructions for setting up call forwarding. Configure where your calls go when you're busy or unavailable.
keywords: call forwarding, forward calls, busy, unavailable

title: Voicemail Setup and Management
summary: Configure voicemail, set up greetings, and manage voicemail settings. Includes visual voicemail and email notifications.

title: Ring Groups Overview
summary: Create and manage ring groups. Add users, set ring strategies (simultaneous, sequential), and configure time-based routing.
keywords: ring group, hunt group, simultaneous ring, sequential

title: Ring Groups and Time-Based Routing
summary: Configure ring groups to route calls differently by time of day. Use schedules to define work hours vs after hours, and assign different destinations (ring groups or departments) to each time block.
keywords: ring group, time-based routing, work hours, after hours, schedule

title: Schedules and Time Blocks
summary: Create schedules to define when your business is open. Schedules use weekdays and time ranges. Time blocks in ring groups reference schedules for work-hours vs after-hours routing.
keywords: schedule, time block, work hours, business hours, weekdays

title: Welcome Menus and DTMF Options
summary: Set up auto-attendant menus with DTMF options. Callers hear a greeting and can press a key to reach departments, ring groups, or extensions.
keywords: welcome menu, auto-attendant, DTMF, IVR, press 1

title: Overflow and Final Tier (Voicemail)
summary: When no one answers in a ring group, calls can overflow to voicemail. The final tier defines what happens when all members fail to answer. Configure department or user voicemail as the overflow destination.
keywords: overflow, final tier, voicemail, no answer, ring group

title: Assigning Phone Numbers to Ring Groups and Menus
summary: Assign a main number or DID to a ring group or welcome menu so incoming calls route correctly. Use the admin portal to set the route type and destination for each phone number.
keywords: assign phone number, main number, DID, ring group, welcome menu, route

title: Call Queues Setup
summary: Set up call queues for your team. Configure agents, wait times, overflow, and queue strategies.
keywords: call queue, ACD, agents, wait time

title: Adding Users and Extensions
summary: Add new users to your net2phone account. Assign extensions, phone numbers, and configure user settings.
keywords: add user, create user, extension, new user

title: Phone Number Porting
summary: Port your existing phone numbers to net2phone. Requirements, timeline, and what to expect during the porting process.
keywords: port, porting, transfer number, keep number
